expectations: Does NOT interview the user — synthesizes from existing context
Explores the codebase to understand current state before writing
Sketches major modules and checks with user before writing the full PRD
Produces a PRD using the template with Problem Statement, Solution, User Stories, Implementation Decisions, Testing Decisions, Out of Scope sections
Uses domain glossary vocabulary from the project throughout the PRD
Actively looks for deep modules that can be tested in isolation
Publishes the PRD to the issue tracker with the ready-for-agent label
Does not include specific file paths or line numbers in Implementation Decisions
